topics.title: Climate Science Breakthroughs
topics.executive_summary: A breakthrough AI system is revealing never-before-seen ocean currents critical to climate forecasting, while a stark new study warns New Orleans must begin planning immediate relocation as rising seas threaten to surround the city by century's end.

stories.headline: AI Unveils Hidden Ocean Currents Critical to Climate Science
stories.body: Scientists developed GOFLOW, an AI tool that turns weather satellite images into detailed maps of fast-moving ocean currents previously impossible to observe. The breakthrough uses existing satellites to reveal small-scale currents key to understanding heat transport, carbon storage, and marine ecosystems without requiring new hardware.

stories.headline: New Orleans Told to Start Relocation Planning Now
stories.body: A Nature study published this week concludes New Orleans has reached a climate 'point of no return' and must begin planning to relocate residents as sea-level rise combined with sinking land will likely surround the city with Gulf waters by the end of this century. Researchers say the transition requires generations of planning and should start immediately.

stories.headline: Airborne Microplastics Now Warming the Atmosphere
stories.body: Colored microplastics floating in the atmosphere are trapping heat at about 16% the warming power of black carbon soot, according to research published Monday in Nature Climate Change. Scientists found darker plastic particles absorb sunlight as they drift globally, creating a previously unrecognized contributor to climate change.
